objective: Stabilize the existing AI Growth OS backend system for production by implementing persistence, container lifecycle management, backup capability, and health monitoring without modifying core execution logic.
decisions: Adopt Docker Compose as the primary lifecycle manager instead of manual scripts
Enable PostgreSQL persistence using Docker volume ai-growth-os_pgdata
Enable Redis persistence using AOF (appendonly yes)
Add restart policy 'unless-stopped' to core services (api, worker, learning, redis, db)
Introduce healthchecks for Redis and PostgreSQL using redis-cli ping and pg_isready
Use depends_on with condition service_healthy to control startup order
Create manual backup system using pg_dump executed inside PostgreSQL container
Expose /health endpoint in FastAPI for service monitoring
Remove reliance on manual process control (pkill, port cleanup, run.sh logic)
Use environment variables for DB and Redis connection instead of hardcoded localhost
Keep architecture unchanged while adding production hardening layers
built_components: Docker Compose infrastructure for API, worker, learning, Redis, PostgreSQL, Prometheus, Grafana, Alertmanager, n8n
Persistent storage layer for PostgreSQL using Docker volume
Persistent storage layer for Redis using AOF and Docker volume
Backup workflow using scripts/backup_db.sh with pg_dump
Docker healthcheck system for Redis and PostgreSQL
Service dependency control using Docker service_healthy condition
Environment-based configuration for DATABASE_URL and REDIS_HOST
inputs: User requirement to avoid breaking existing system
User-reported issues: data loss in PostgreSQL, Redis connection failures, Docker confusion
User requirement for clean UX and stable container execution
Existing system with API, worker, learning services already functional
outputs: Validated Docker-based execution flow (API → Redis → Worker → Execution)
Operational system with persistence, restart safety, and monitoring readiness
notes: System is operational with production hardening applied. Persistence, restart policies, and healthchecks are implemented. Backup exists but is manual. Monitoring stack is present but alerting is not configured. Security for admin endpoints and restore workflow are not implemented.
